PATIENT CARE ENCOUNTER

texts.label: text
texts.orig:
Department of Veterans Affairs
 Health System Design & Development
texts.text:
Department of Veterans Affairs
 Health System Design & Development

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Patient Care Encounter (PCE) patch is part of the Patient Financial Services System (PFSS) project. PFSS patches are being released on various schedules. Some patch functionality will not be active until a new PFSS switch is activated during final implementation. PFSS will initially be implemented at select pilot sites ONLY.
texts.text: The Patient Care Encounter (PCE) patch is part of the Patient Financial Services System (PFSS) project. PFSS patches are being released on various schedules. Some patch functionality will not be active until a new PFSS switch is activated during final implementation. PFSS will initially be implemented at select pilot sites ONLY.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The purpose of the PFSS project is to prepare the Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture () environment for the implementation of a commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) billing replacement system. The project consists of the implementation of the billing replacement system, business process improvements, and enhancements to  to support integration with the COTS billing replacement system. Significant changes to  legacy systems and ancillary packages are necessary.
texts.text: The purpose of the PFSS project is to prepare the Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture () environment for the implementation of a commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) billing replacement system. The project consists of the implementation of the billing replacement system, business process improvements, and enhancements to  to support integration with the COTS billing replacement system. Significant changes to  legacy systems and ancillary packages are necessary.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: These PFSS software components are not operational until the PFSS On/Off Switch, distributed with patch IB*2*260, is set to "ON". The ability for the local site to set the switch to "ON" will be provided at the appropriate time with the release of a subsequent Integrated Billing (IB) patch. For more information about the PFSS project, review the documentation accompanying this patch and refer to the following website: REDACTED/.
texts.text: These PFSS software components are not operational until the PFSS On/Off Switch, distributed with patch IB*2*260, is set to "ON". The ability for the local site to set the switch to "ON" will be provided at the appropriate time with the release of a subsequent Integrated Billing (IB) patch. For more information about the PFSS project, review the documentation accompanying this patch and refer to the following website: REDACTED/.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The PCE PFSS 1B project will add the following new functionality to the PCE Application:
texts.text: The PCE PFSS 1B project will add the following new functionality to the PCE Application:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A new field, PFSS Account Reference, which is used by the external billing system to attach charges for 1
texts.text: A new field, PFSS Account Reference, which is used by the external billing system to attach charges for 1

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: PCE now determines if there is a PFSS Account Reference available either by being passed-in with a DATA2PCE API call, or by association with a scheduled appointment, or through association with an order.
texts.text: PCE now determines if there is a PFSS Account Reference available either by being passed-in with a DATA2PCE API call, or by association with a scheduled appointment, or through association with an order.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: If no PFSS Account Reference is available, PCE will call the Integrated Billing subsystem (IBB) Get Account API to create a new PFSS Account Reference.
texts.text: If no PFSS Account Reference is available, PCE will call the Integrated Billing subsystem (IBB) Get Account API to create a new PFSS Account Reference.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A new field, Department Code, which defines the service area for a charge.
texts.text: A new field, Department Code, which defines the service area for a charge.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A new field, PFSS Charge ID, which uniquely identifies each charge item in the external billing system.
texts.text: A new field, PFSS Charge ID, which uniquely identifies each charge item in the external billing system.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Changes to the DATA2PCE API and Visit Tracking to accommodate the new fields.
texts.text: Changes to the DATA2PCE API and Visit Tracking to accommodate the new fields.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A new filer routine used to file charges into the Integrated Billing subsystem (IBB) cache buffer
texts.text: A new filer routine used to file charges into the Integrated Billing subsystem (IBB) cache buffer

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: New dynamically created Charge Message SC/EI classifications for procedures that are auto-populated based on the procedure clinical indicators and are filed with the charge message into the IBB cache buffer.
texts.text: New dynamically created Charge Message SC/EI classifications for procedures that are auto-populated based on the procedure clinical indicators and are filed with the charge message into the IBB cache buffer.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A new post-installation routine to activate the Order/Entry Result Reporting package in the Visit Tracking Parameters (#150.9) file.
texts.text: A new post-installation routine to activate the Order/Entry Result Reporting package in the Visit Tracking Parameters (#150.9) file.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The new features, functions, and enhancements of the PCE package are grouped and discussed in detail in the following sections.
texts.text: The new features, functions, and enhancements of the PCE package are grouped and discussed in detail in the following sections.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There are no new additions or changes to any of the menu options in the PCE package.
texts.text: There are no new additions or changes to any of the menu options in the PCE package.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There are no new reports or changes to the reports in the PCE package.
texts.text: There are no new reports or changes to the reports in the PCE package.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This section contains new or changed files and fields in the PCE package.
texts.text: This section contains new or changed files and fields in the PCE package.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There are no new files added to the PCE package.
texts.text: There are no new files added to the PCE package.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Based on the associated clinical indicators, the Charge Message SC/EI classifications will be auto-populated according to the following rules:
texts.text: Based on the associated clinical indicators, the Charge Message SC/EI classifications will be auto-populated according to the following rules:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If the SC/EI for at least one ICD-9 is "Yes", then the Charge Message SC/EI will automatically be set to "Yes".
texts.text: If the SC/EI for at least one ICD-9 is "Yes", then the Charge Message SC/EI will automatically be set to "Yes".

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If the SC/EI for all ICD-9's is "No", then the Charge Message SC/EI will automatically be set to "No".
texts.text: If the SC/EI for all ICD-9's is "No", then the Charge Message SC/EI will automatically be set to "No".

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If at least one ICD-9 is missing an SC/EI and none of the other ICD-9's SC/EI is "Yes", use the Encounter Level SC/EI.
texts.text: If at least one ICD-9 is missing an SC/EI and none of the other ICD-9's SC/EI is "Yes", use the Encounter Level SC/EI.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Add a dotted variable to the calling parameters which represents the PFSS Account Reference.
texts.text: Add a dotted variable to the calling parameters which represents the PFSS Account Reference.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: $$DATA2PCE^PXAPI(INPUTROOT,PKG,SOURCE,.VISIT,USER,ERRDISP,.ERRARRAY,PPEDIT,.ERRPROB, .ACCOUNT)   This is a function which will  return a value identifying the status of the call. Data that is processed by PCE will be posted on the PXK VISIT DATA EVENT protocol.
texts.text: $$DATA2PCE^PXAPI(INPUTROOT,PKG,SOURCE,.VISIT,USER,ERRDISP,.ERRARRAY,PPEDIT,.ERRPROB, .ACCOUNT)   This is a function which will  return a value identifying the status of the call. Data that is processed by PCE will be posted on the PXK VISIT DATA EVENT protocol.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: (Optional) A dotted variable name, where ACCOUNT is the PFSS Account Reference associated with the data being passed by the calling application. Each PFSS Account Reference represents an internal entry number in the PFSS ACCOUNT file (# 375).
texts.text: (Optional) A dotted variable name, where ACCOUNT is the PFSS Account Reference associated with the data being passed by the calling application. Each PFSS Account Reference represents an internal entry number in the PFSS ACCOUNT file (# 375).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Add a new DEPARTMENT subscript to the procedure level for the DATA2PCE API.
texts.text: Add a new DEPARTMENT subscript to the procedure level for the DATA2PCE API.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The "PROCEDURE" node may have multiple entries (i). Only active CPT/HCPCS codes will be accepted. The "PROCEDURE" node documents the procedure(s), the number of times the procedure was performed, the diagnosis the procedure is associated with and the narrative that describes the procedure. It also enables documentation of the provider who performed the procedure, the date/time the procedure was performed and any comments that are associated with the procedure. To delete the entire "PROCEDURE" entry, set the "DELETE" node to 1.
texts.text: The "PROCEDURE" node may have multiple entries (i). Only active CPT/HCPCS codes will be accepted. The "PROCEDURE" node documents the procedure(s), the number of times the procedure was performed, the diagnosis the procedure is associated with and the narrative that describes the procedure. It also enables documentation of the provider who performed the procedure, the date/time the procedure was performed and any comments that are associated with the procedure. To delete the entire "PROCEDURE" entry, set the "DELETE" node to 1.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: "PROCEDURE",i,"DEPARTMENT")	A 3-digit code that defines the service area. Missing Department Codes will be assigned a Department Code. The Department Code will be the Stop Code associated (in the HOSPITAL LOCATION file, #44) with the Hospital Location of the patient visit.
texts.text: "PROCEDURE",i,"DEPARTMENT")	A 3-digit code that defines the service area. Missing Department Codes will be assigned a Department Code. The Department Code will be the Stop Code associated (in the HOSPITAL LOCATION file, #44) with the Hospital Location of the patient visit.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Visit Tracking is a utility that can be used by a variety of  modules (usually via PCE), with potential benefits for clinical, administrative, and fiscal applications. Visit Tracking will allow  packages to link an event to a patient visit entry, thereby linking that event to any number of events occurring throughout the hospital during the patient's outpatient and/or inpatient episode.
texts.text: Visit Tracking is a utility that can be used by a variety of  modules (usually via PCE), with potential benefits for clinical, administrative, and fiscal applications. Visit Tracking will allow  packages to link an event to a patient visit entry, thereby linking that event to any number of events occurring throughout the hospital during the patient's outpatient and/or inpatient episode.
